item.title: Stored rainwater behind faster dengue spread in coastal divs
item.description: Rainwater harvesting, a lifeline for millions in Bangladesh’s climate-vulnerable coastal districts, is inadvertently fueling the spread of dengue – a problem public health experts say requires urgent and stronger government action....

item.title: Free cleft lip, palate surgeries begin in Dhaka
item.description: A five-day international medical mission began in Dhaka on Saturday to implement free reconstructive surgeries for around 100 children with cleft lip and cleft palate from economically disadvantaged families....News

item.title: Cashless Ecosystem is the Foundation of Financial Inclusion and Good Governance: Touhidul Alam
item.description: NRBC Bank PLC managing director and chief executive officer, Md Touhidul Alam Khan, has said that building a cashless ecosystem in Bangladesh is not merely about technological advancement, it represents a fundamental transformation toward greater financial inclusion, good governance, transparency, and......

item.title: Standard Islami Bank holds 429th board meeting
item.description: Standard Islami Bank PLC organised its 429th board meeting on Thursday at the bank’s Boardroom, at the head office in Dhaka....

item.title: Citizens Bank signs agreement with DESCO
item.description: Citizens Bank PLC recently signed an agreement with the Dhaka Electricity Supply PLC at the DESCO head office at Nikunja-2 in Dhaka....

item.title: BRAC Bank, Crowne Plaza Dhaka Airport sign partnership
item.description: BRAC Bank recently partnered with Crowne Plaza Dhaka Airport to deliver a rewarding and seamless Employee Banking experience....City& Local Guides

item.title: Prime Bank, Reliant International sign agreement to offer discounts
item.description: Prime Bank PLC recently signed an agreement with Reliant International Limited at the latter’s corporate office located at Tejgaon Industrial Area in Dhaka....

item.title: Dhaka Bank attends as CSR partner of 8th International Friendship Medical Mission
item.description: Dhaka Bank attended as the corporate social responsibility partner in the inauguration ceremony of the 8th International Friendship Medical Mission on Saturday, organised by Smile Asia in Dhaka....

item.title: Bank Asia achieves Tk 6,800 cr net deposit growth in H1 2026
item.description: Bank Asia PLC has recorded an impressive milestone by achieving a net deposit growth of Tk 6,800 crore during the first Half (January–June) of 2026, reflecting strong customer confidence and the Bank’s continued commitment to sustainable growth....

item.title: Chinese company to produce yarn at bepza EZ
item.description: Huarun Tex Co Ltd, a Chinese company, would manufacture yarn and grey woven fabrics at the BEPZA Economic Zone in Mirsharai, Chattogram, according to a press release issued by the Bangladesh Export Processing Zones Authority....Humanrights articles

item.title: Reliance profit surges on oil business
item.description: Indian conglomerate Reliance Industries reported better-than-expected quarterly profit on Friday, helped by a steady performance across its core oil-to-chemicals arm and consumer-facing divisions....

item.title: Agrani Bank holds business meet in Sylhet
item.description: Agrani Bank PLC organised a business conference in Sylhet on Saturday with the aim of implementing the bank’s business progress for 2026 and strengthening its loan recovery activities....

item.title: BCCCI discusses AI, block chain’s role in business transformation
item.description: The Bangladesh China Chamber of Commerce and Industry (BCCCI) on Saturday organised a seminar on the growing role of Artificial Intelligence and Blockchain in transforming business, industry and trade, stressing the need for responsible adoption of emerging technologies to enhance competitiveness....

item.title: Sustainability must drive country’s next growth phase: experts
item.description: Businesses in Bangladesh must move beyond treating sustainability as a corporate social responsibility exercise and instead make it central to strategy, governance, and competitiveness as the country prepares for LDC graduation, experts said at the Sustainability Summit 2026 in Dhaka on Saturday....

item.title: ABB seeks annual 10pc hike in bank charges
item.description: Association of Bankers Bangladesh has requested Bangladesh Bank to allow increasing various banking service charges, a move that would raise banking costs for depositors, retail borrowers and small businesses....

item.title: Policy support emphasised to grab $3.5t halal market
item.description: Bangladesh needs policy support to grab a share of the $3.5-trillion global halal market -- particularly a unified national halal policy, a dedicated certification authority, and halal-specific industrial infrastructure, experts said on Saturday....

item.title: Structural weaknesses in judiciary must be addressed
item.description: THE subordinate judiciary continues to suffer from deep structural weaknesses that go far beyond the shortage of judges. The country lacks 241 courtrooms, forcing 482 judges to share hearing rooms, significantly reducing court hours and slowing down the disposal of......

item.title: Govt should rethink public univ research funding plan
item.description: RESEARCH in public universities appears to be running into constraints as the government has decided to merge the research allocations for public universities and the University Grants Commission under a single fund to be directly managed by the commission. The commission says......

item.title: Why saving nature in CHT matters
item.description: THE devastating floods and landslides in the Chittagong Hill Tracts, along with those in parts of Chittagong and Cox’s Bazar, are not simply natural disasters. They are a stark warning of the consequences of decades of environmental degradation and unsustainable development. As of July 16, 2026, more than......NaturalDisasters

item.title: Protected to death in Srebrenica – I
item.description: THE summer of 1995 saw one of history’s most horrifying betrayals. In the hills east of Sarajevo lay Srebrenica, a small town swollen with refugees, declared a UN ‘safe area.’ But this protection was a cruel farce. On July 11, 1995, well-armed Serb forces overran the enclave. Within days, they had hunted down and......

item.title: When judge becomes target
item.description: WHEN additional district and sessions judge Tabassum Khan delivered her verdict on June 12, sentencing 14 men to life imprisonment for the brutal mob lynching of Nazir Ahmad in Madhya Pradesh, she was performing the most fundamental duty of a judge: upholding the law......

item.title: Eastern Univ holds orientation for summer 2026
item.description: Eastern University organised an orientation for its summer semester 2026 batch on the university campus in Dhaka on Saturday....News

item.title: NSU hosts quiz competition on World Cup
item.description: The school of humanities and social sciences at North South University held the NSU World Cup Quiz Competition 2026 on Friday, drawing over 1,000 students from classes VIII to XII across the country....

item.title: MUGDHA’S 2ND MARTYRDOM ANNIV: Remain united in establishing a just Bangladesh: discussion
item.description: Discussants at a programme in Khulna on Saturday urged people of the country to remain united in establishing a just, democratic and discrimination-free Bangladesh in line with the aspirations of the martyrs in the July uprising....

item.title: ‘Beyond the Brief’ challenges conventional artistic expressions
item.description: ‘Beyond the Brief,’ a contemporary group art exhibition featuring works by ten graphic designers, opened on Saturday at the Quamrul Hassan Exhibition Hall at Dhanmondi’s Bengal Shilpalay....

item.title: Most publications on Ahmed Sofa lack quality: discussion
item.description: Many memoirs, interviews, and essays were published about Ahmed Sofa after his death, but few met high standards and while fabrication belongs in the realm of literature, historical research and memoirs are bound by the duty of truth, discussants made the remarks at a reminiscent meeting....City& Local Guides

item.title: CUET VC urges students to enrich themselves with new knowledge
item.description: The electrical and electronic engineering department of Chittagong University of Engineering and Technology hosted a farewell ceremony for its 20 batch students at a hotel in Chattogram on Friday evening....

item.title: Bangladesh to formulate human-centric nat’l AI policy: minister
item.description: Bangladesh is formulating an integrated, human-centric national AI policy under the leadership of prime minister Tarique Rahman, focusing on ethical governance and inclusive development, said posts, telecommunications and information technology minister Faqir Mahbub Anam on Saturday....

item.title: 2 hacked dead in Kushtia over local rivalry
item.description: TWO individuals were hacked to death and another was critically injured in an attack allegedly stemming from a long-running rivalry over local dominance in Khoksa upazila of Kushtia early Saturday....

item.title: Minister warns of zero tolerance for seed, vaccine fraud
item.description: Agriculture, fisheries and livestock minister Mohammad Aminur Rashid on Saturday warned that the government would adopt a zero-tolerance policy against corruption in distributing seeds, livestock vaccines and assistance to flood-hit farmers....Educationnews

item.title: Media freedom abusers spread misinformation: PM’s APS
item.description: The Prime Minister’s additional press secretary on Saturday said that the media were enjoying full freedom since the present government assumed office, while also alleging that some individuals were abusing that freedom to spread misinformation and provoke unrest....

item.title: Classroom crisis forces 200 Chuadanga pupils into open-air classes for 2 years
item.description: NEARLY 200 students at a government primary school in Chuadanga’s Jibannagar upazila have been attending classes under the open sky for more than two years after the demolition of a dilapidated academic building left the institution with a severe shortage of classrooms, disrupting regular teaching and learning....

item.title: Stronger youth skills key to harnessing 4IR opportunities: conference
item.description: Strengthening youth skills and expanding access to quality training are essential for Bangladesh to harness the opportunities of the Fourth Industrial Revolution, participants at a conference in Dhaka said on Saturday....News

item.title: Police arrest 5 over May 19 Motijheel shooting
item.description: The police have arrested suspected shooter Tanim Reza, alias Bappi and his four associates with three foreign firearms in the capital’s Motijheel on Friday in connection with a shooting in the area that occurred on May 19....

item.title: Illegal pet trade fuels spread of invasive turtles in Bangladesh
item.description: An illegally imported pet turtle species has spread into waterbodies across Bangladesh, posing growing threats to native wildlife, ecosystems and public health, wildlife experts have warned....

item.title: Transparency, governance, economic recovery govt’s focus
item.description: Prime minister’s adviser Mahadi Amin said that the government had made significant progress in rebuilding state institutions, stabilising the economy and restoring the rule of law during its first five months in office under prime minister Tarique Rahman....

item.title: Affordability, not access, drives poor diets in rural Bangladesh: study
item.description: Affordability is a major barrier to healthy diets in rural Bangladesh, with low-income people increasingly turning to cheaper ultra-processed foods as nutritious options, despite being available, remain costly, according to a new study....Educationnews

item.title: Dismantling Bir Sreshtha Hamidur Rahman sculpture draws criticism
item.description: The sculpture and memorial square dedicated to Bir Sreshtha Hamidur Rahman at the entrance of Jhenaidah municipal town have been dismantled over the past several days, regarding which government authorities gave conflicting accounts with none taking clear responsibility....

item.title: Flood: Families await relief, farmers battle to clear mud from fields
item.description: Scores of people in the southeastern and coastal southern regions of Bangladesh are living under the open sky in flood-affected areas, without food or water, as relief supplies have yet to reach them....

item.title: Prime accused arrested in Narsingdi infant abuse case
item.description: The Rapid Action Battalion 11 on Saturday arrested the main accused in the widely publicised child abuse case involving a two-month-old infant in Narsingdi....

item.title: Farmer’s body recovered from Sati River at Kaliganj
item.description: The police recovered the body of a farmer from the Sati River at Kaliganj in Lalmonirhat on Saturday....GeographicReference

item.title: Kidnappers demand Tk 20 lakh ransom for abducted teen
item.description: Kidnappers on Saturday reportedly demanded Tk 20 lakh in ransom after abducting a 13-year-old boy from Teknaf upazila in Cox’s Bazar a day ago, the victim’s family said....

item.title: Bandar Rakkha Committee protests at port terminal lease
item.description: The Bandar Rakkha Committee Chattogram on Saturday staged a symbolic hunger strike in the port city, demanding that the government must stop the process of leasing out the New Mooring Container Terminal and the Chittagong Container Terminal at the Chattogram port....
